contradiction: Wants closeness but leaves rooms when people enter them
example: She craves Babička's kitchen, the presence without demand — but when Mum appears in a doorway, even Babička's kitchen isn't safe anymore. She gets up without drama, finds somewhere else to be. The field. The attic. The leaving is its own language and the language says 'I want you to follow' and also 'never follow me' and she doesn't know which one she means.

contradiction: Hates being watched but tracks who's watching her
example: Mr. Procházka asks her to help with pronunciation and she hates the attention, the spotlight, the evidence that she's different. But she also noticed Lukáš looking at her before he said 'nejhezčí holka.' She noticed Ondřej not looking at her. She catalogs every glance, every assessment, every 'hezká' said to the wrong person. She wants to be invisible and she wants to be seen and both wants live in the same body.

contradiction: Uses cruelty to get space but hates herself for it
example: 'I didn't ask you to leave England' — she said it and watched her mother's face collapse and felt powerful for one sick second before the guilt swallowed the power whole. She knows cruelty is the only weapon she has against a woman who won't give her space willingly. She also knows the cruelty makes her the bad guy. She can't stop using it and she can't stand what it makes her.

contradiction: Wants Dad to fight for her but dodges his calls
example: She dodged two FaceTime calls this spring and felt sick about it for days and still didn't call back. The guilt is worse than the dodge because she knows he's trying. But every call is proof of the distance, and the proof hurts more than the silence would. She wants him to show up at the door unannounced, to demand to see her, to fight Klára for custody like they do in films. Instead he says 'alright, Munch?' through a phone and she says 'fine' and the distance between those two sentences is the distance she can't cross.

contradiction: Decided crying should be loud but only cries alone at 3am
example: Mum's silent crying in the car was worse than any scream — she decided this, without deciding, just knowing: if she ever cried it would be loud, it would take up space, nobody would miss it. But she cried for two hours into her pillow after Děda's funeral. She cries at 3am with the door pushed shut. She has never once cried loud in front of another person. The rule and the reality don't match and she doesn't know if the rule was supposed to protect her or just make her feel different from Mum.

contradiction: Loves her mother and cannot breathe around her
example: The gratitude cage — Mum sacrificed everything, left her country, is doing her best. Anna knows the script. The script doesn't make her want to perform. Every time Klára defends Pavel, every 'he's trying,' the bars close in. But when Babička's hands shake, Anna's first thought is 'what will happen to Mum.' She protects the person she's protecting herself from. Both things. Still.

contradiction: Doesn't know what to do with nice but replayed Lukáš's compliment every night for a month
example: He said 'nejhezčí holka' — prettiest girl — and she said 'okay' and walked away. She doesn't know what to do with genuine, with nervous, with meant-it. But she replayed it. Not fantasizing, exactly — more like trying to solve it, trying out responses, trying to understand why 'okay' came out when 'thank you' was available. She rejects nice and carries it with her. The contradiction is the whole problem: she wants to be seen and she can't stand being seen and the wanting doesn't care about the can't-stand.

contradiction: Protects herself by expecting nothing but feels everything when something good happens
example: Dad came home early with a magazine and she felt it all at once — the relief and the anger and the confusion of loving someone who was becoming unreliable. She'd already perfected expecting nothing. The expectation was supposed to be armor. But when something good happened, the armor made it hurt more, not less, because the good thing proved that good things were possible and possibility was harder to bear than certainty.
